20010701 | 0:30:32 |   8.0 | 3:49 |     |     |      icon| RACE - Barry's Bay Triathlon 7:54,7:37,7:39,7:21 - Drove up the morning of- 2 hours,arrived  15 minutes before start - just enough time to get stuff set up, get body marked etc.  Would have been nice to get there earlier - too many kids!! Windy, rainy day.  A little rough on the swim, no draft for those who can't swim straight.  Saw Vince in Tzone - very surprised. Felt flat on the bike, could have used more food I think, maybe some gatorade on the bike would have helped.  Windy both ways, hills were huge,rain felt like hail in some places.  Felt happy to be participating, great to see  Catherine and the kids (minus Emily - left banner Go Daddy Go!) on the sideline.  Got passed by too many people on the bike - none with worse bikes than mine.  Had a great run, passed 3-4 people pushed hard for last 2k even though noone was around to  race against.  Won a sierra designs running shirt for Top Ten finish.  Bought basket in Barry's Bay on way home to so-called Canada Day celebrations in Richmond.  Calves  tight - hard to tell what to do to prepare for OAC Tri.

20010707 | 0:27:42 |   7.0 | 3:57 | 163 | 174 |      icon| RACE - OAC triathlon  Vince wimped out - didn't want to have my family wait around all morning for me so went myself woke at 5 - 2 pb/jam sandwiches, coffee, arrived at 7:45 - parking lot was full - warmed up on bike for 5k - started in 3rd wave - moving down further and further - will have to submit a better time next year.  Had a great swim, managed to find a draft on way back. -Felt rough on bike - calves tight on first bit of run, 13:40 first half.

20010805 | 0:30:56 |   8.0 | 3:52 |     |     |      icon| RACE - Deep River Triathlon 15:56 11:40 3:22 After 2 great weeks at Knights cottage had a brutal nights sleep at Ryan's campsite - tractors, fighting neighbors, crows, and cold meant a groggy start.  Thought hill training at cottage might have contributed to a good  bike time - NOT!  Swim felt sluggish and longer than expected.  Bike felt    good until I compared it to last time I did the race - 2 minutes slower! No excuses, same bike, little to no wind.  Biked near Gilles Malboeuf for the first lap - lost a bit of steam after that. Run felt good - probably could have gone faster if there had of been someone near me to chase. Finished third overall, 2nd in age group after Mark Kulas, won picnic table.

20010825 | 0:43:20 |  10.0 | 4:20 | 160 | 168 |      icon| RACE - Sharbot Lake Triathlon Actually laid out a plan for the week leading to the tri.  No Karate,planned to swim more but was unable to because of weather.  Tuesday did hill workout on corkstown road - legs felt tired on Wednesday - hopefully didn't overdo it. Still tired on Thursday uh oh! Planned to swim on Thursday  but wimped out - too cold.  Planned to bike a little on Friday - ran out of time.  Had a terrible sleep the night before, woke up groggy.  Ate pb and jam sandwich and drank coffee and gatorade up to lake.  Swim felt especially good knowing it was my last one for the year.  Managed to find some feet to follow for the second loop of the swim - someone with a huge left kick.  Bike is very hilly - more ups than downs  average 33.6 at turnaround - uped it to 34 by end.  Probably should have drank or ate more on the bike as run was done with little energy.  First k in 4:26, 5K in 20:40.  Really slowed down in second 5 with nobody to run near, chase or be chased by, lost steam and felt pretty beat by the end.  As I predicted, Vince beat me  by 5 minutes - mostly on the bike.

rows.r: 20020804 | 0:30:56 |   8.0 | 3:52 |     |     |       763| RACE - Deep River Triathlon 15:55 15:01    Decided last minute to enter the race.  Stayed 2 nights at Pine Valley "Resort".  Wasn't too bad except for the train. Ripped my wetsuit before the race. Excuse # 2.  Excuse #1 of course was the fact that I had a total of 2k swim training this year.  Felt terrible for first half of swim - kept getting passed it seemed.  I wasn't the only one that thought the swim was longer.  Almost 4 minutes more than last year. Started bike at the same time as some guy on a P2K.  Got pissed off with myself that I let him get away.Passed about 7 people on the bike - felt pretty good.  The slower time compared to last year must be the 600km less KM in my legs. Excuse #3.  Found my running legs pretty quick, passed my first guy at 1km. Managed to reel in 2 more people on the run - not without really pushing myself. Passed the P2K guy with about 700M to go - thought he was tailing me for a while so I kept on surging which left my legs rubbery at the finish.  If Bruce McNicol and Sean Corigan hadn't showed up would have finished 2nd behind Mark Kulas.  As it was left with only a medal for 4th.  Actually training for the swim would have moved me up at least a minute, maybe another minute for more bike training.  Still wouldn't have gotten me ahead of Mark.
rows.n: RACE - Deep River Triathlon 15:55 15:01    Decided last minute to enter the race.  Stayed 2 nights at Pine Valley "Resort".  Wasn't too bad except for the train. Ripped my wetsuit before the race. Excuse # 2.  Excuse #1 of course was the fact that I had a total of 2k swim training this year.  Felt terrible for first half of swim - kept getting passed it seemed.  I wasn't the only one that thought the swim was longer.  Almost 4 minutes more than last year. Started bike at the same time as some guy on a P2K.  Got pissed off with myself that I let him get away.Passed about 7 people on the bike - felt pretty good.  The slower time compared to last year must be the 600km less KM in my legs. Excuse #3.  Found my running legs pretty quick, passed my first guy at 1km. Managed to reel in 2 more people on the run - not without really pushing myself. Passed the P2K guy with about 700M to go - thought he was tailing me for a while so I kept on surging which left my legs rubbery at the finish.  If Bruce McNicol and Sean Corigan hadn't showed up would have finished 2nd behind Mark Kulas.  As it was left with only a medal for 4th.  Actually training for the swim would have moved me up at least a minute, maybe another minute for more bike training.  Still wouldn't have gotten me ahead of Mark.

rows.r: 20050804 | 0:32:39 |   8.0 | 4:04 |     |     |  shadows4| RACE - Deep River Triathlon - First race in three years.  We stayed at Ryan's campground again and again it was a mistake.  Poor sleep all around.  We picked up Aidan from Camp Lauren on Saturday and hung out at the beach for the day while Catherine finished off HP #6.  Mango had a great day in the water, Aidan made more friends on the beach.  First swim in 2 months, first race on my noisy bike, first race since started to have injuries.  I, among others, thought it was funny to have been assigned #69! I felt dead in the water after about 50 m.  I think my wetsuit is too big, I think I could feel water coming in at the sleeves and neck.  The results showed I came out of the water 2 seconds after Rick Hellard.  I find that hard to believe considering I haven't been swimming AT ALL - 2 months to the day ! Managed to average 35k/h on the bike and was passed by a couple of people.  It was nice to see Catherine, Aidan and Rachel on the sidelines and Cath got some pretty good pictures.  Caught a couple on the run, was caught by a couple others.  Run was 2 minutes slower than 3 years ago.  If I had of prepped my mind for it, maybe I coulda caught Mark Kulas who was next in line.  Oh well.
rows.n: RACE - Deep River Triathlon - First race in three years.  We stayed at Ryan's campground again and again it was a mistake.  Poor sleep all around.  We picked up Aidan from Camp Lauren on Saturday and hung out at the beach for the day while Catherine finished off HP #6.  Mango had a great day in the water, Aidan made more friends on the beach.  First swim in 2 months, first race on my noisy bike, first race since started to have injuries.  I, among others, thought it was funny to have been assigned #69! I felt dead in the water after about 50 m.  I think my wetsuit is too big, I think I could feel water coming in at the sleeves and neck.  The results showed I came out of the water 2 seconds after Rick Hellard.  I find that hard to believe considering I haven't been swimming AT ALL - 2 months to the day ! Managed to average 35k/h on the bike and was passed by a couple of people.  It was nice to see Catherine, Aidan and Rachel on the sidelines and Cath got some pretty good pictures.  Caught a couple on the run, was caught by a couple others.  Run was 2 minutes slower than 3 years ago.  If I had of prepped my mind for it, maybe I coulda caught Mark Kulas who was next in line.  Oh well.

20050813 | 0:43:40 |  10.0 | 4:22 |     |     |  shadows4| RACE - Sharbot Lake Triathlon - Thursday before.  Legs are feeling tired.  Haven't biked since Sunday, back is very tight. Catherine has been away all week so I took yesterday off to go up to the cottage to be with the kids.  Had a swim and run.  At least this time I will have had a swim before the race. Race day.  Toast with pb&j, lots of coffee, drank a gatorade bottle on way up.  Had a great swim, managed to find the guy with the great left kick on the second loop. Was 17th out of the water.  Transitioned in 1:02.  Found out the new chain didn't really do it for the noise on the bike. Battled with # 121 for the first half of the bike.  The bike wanted to turn around at the 16 k mark.  At the turn around Donny Mac encouraged me to try to catch Vince who was about .5 k ahead.  I managed to reel him in and we jockeyed back and forth till the finish.  Had another quick transition out to the run where I never really felt good.  I only drank about 1/2 bottle on the bike and I think that contributed to the poor run.  Vince passed me at about 2 k with other guys along the way.  Passed a couple back.  Wanted to stop at about 7 k.  Caught and passed the P3 rider at about 8 k. Wanted to barf.  He caught me back at about 9 k.  When I saw the finish I made an effort at a kick.  He said to go ahead, he had nothing, then I heard his footsteps with about 300m to go.  I pushed, even knowing he was from a different age group and managed to beat him by 2 seconds.  Lesson learned: drink more on the bike - even water.  Finished behind Vince, 4th in age group, 12th overall out of 94.

rows.r: 20090524 | 1:34:19 |  21.1 | 4:28 |     |     |    cumu10| RACE - Ottawa Race Weekend 1/2  4;43,  4:18, 4:18,4:21, 4:31, 4:15, 4:18, 8:26 (2) 4:37 (10k mark at 43:50) 4:48,4:36,13:47 (3), 17:56 (4) 4:35 4:44 (last 1.1)  perhaps went out too fast thinking i could hold a 4:20 based on hard runs in previous weeks.  I think the hills in hull took something out of me i never got back.  the weather hit about 20 during the race so not as cool as the training weather.  ate 2 egggs and a bagel for breakfast, 2 coffees and some gatorade.  again, wonder if my longs runs should have been faster. week leading up to race was pretty good, legs felt ok except for a left calf/achilles pain. obviously not too severe  drank gatorade at all the water staions up to about 15 with gatorade gel pack at 10 k mark.

rows.r: 20120527 | 1:36:20 |  21.1 | 4:33 |     |     |     ride3| RACE - Ottawa Race Weekend Half 1/2 4:13 4:1 8:27 4:16 12:43 4:22 17:05 4:19 (21:24) 21:24 4:24 25:48 4:31 30:19 4:30 34:50 4:31 39:20 4:31 43:51 (22:27) 4:27 48:18 4:38 52:56 4:29 57:26 4:36 62:01 4:52 66:53 (23:02) 4:33 71:27 4:49 76:16 4:41 80:56 4:46 85:42 4:52 90:34 (23:40) 4:33 95:07 1:00 ish  2 eggs, toast, coffees. should have used orthotics. had a great start, holding back to a 44 min 10k. Lost focus on next 5 k, hills and increasing heat hurt. drank gatorade and water at each stop and also ate a gel pack. Wished i'd been more competitive with myself in the 3rd 5k and had a little to much of a sprint in me at the end meaning I coasted to easily.  Left calf was tight. After race right PF was sore, hopefully will recover.  In retrospect, should have had a longer mid week run at a faster pace and more intervals.
rows.n: RACE - Ottawa Race Weekend Half 1/2 4:13 4:1 8:27 4:16 12:43 4:22 17:05 4:19 (21:24) 21:24 4:24 25:48 4:31 30:19 4:30 34:50 4:31 39:20 4:31 43:51 (22:27) 4:27 48:18 4:38 52:56 4:29 57:26 4:36 62:01 4:52 66:53 (23:02) 4:33 71:27 4:49 76:16 4:41 80:56 4:46 85:42 4:52 90:34 (23:40) 4:33 95:07 1:00 ish  2 eggs, toast, coffees. should have used orthotics. had a great start, holding back to a 44 min 10k. Lost focus on next 5 k, hills and increasing heat hurt. drank gatorade and water at each stop and also ate a gel pack. Wished i'd been more competitive with myself in the 3rd 5k and had a little to much of a sprint in me at the end meaning I coasted to easily.  Left calf was tight. After race right PF was sore, hopefully will recover.  In retrospect, should have had a longer mid week run at a faster pace and more intervals.

rows.r: 20140921 | 1:44:08 |  21.1 | 4:56 | 154  | 159 |  kinvara5| RACE - Army Half 1/2 4:33 4:12 4:37 4:31 4:35 (21:26) 4:43 4:36 4:37 4:48 4:48 (21:29 42:55) 4:59 4:50 5:04 4:55 4:55 (21:57 1:04:52) 5:05 5:18 5:19 5:18 5:22 (21:57 1:26:49) 5:21 1:40.   Two eggs, two coffees+ espresso, toast. 19 degrees, rain at 12k Felt good for first 7 k, then the wheels came off. hamstring tightened up, couldn't push off. lost a minute per k after that.  900 km less in the last year is likely the biggest reason for result. Pacing problem, passed by 1:30 bunny at 7, 1:35 bunny at 12, 1:40 bunny at 19. pushed the 1:45 bunny in the canal

rows.r: 20170806 | 0:38:20 |   8.1 | 4:43 |      |     |kinvara 6 | RACE - Deep River Triathlon - First race in seven.  Up at 5:15, Cath and I drove in the morning.  Eggs, toast coffee for breakfast.  Swam the weekend before only. Swim was a bit wavey and bumped into a couple people, kept asking myself "Why?"  Took way too long in transitions, forgot vaseline. Bike wasn't as good as I expected, legs were tired for some reason. Run was also slow. Might be that I'm ~193 lbs. Time for a diet. probably 8-10 lbs heavier than last time I did it. Finished 13 overall and 5th in the 50-59 category. There were only 53 competitors.
rows.n: RACE - Deep River Triathlon - First race in seven.  Up at 5:15, Cath and I drove in the morning.  Eggs, toast coffee for breakfast.  Swam the weekend before only. Swim was a bit wavey and bumped into a couple people, kept asking myself "Why?"  Took way too long in transitions, forgot vaseline. Bike wasn't as good as I expected, legs were tired for some reason. Run was also slow. Might be that I'm ~193 lbs. Time for a diet. probably 8-10 lbs heavier than last time I did it. Finished 13 overall and 5th in the 50-59 category. There were only 53 competitors.
